title: Infinite Carousel
description:
Build an **Infinite Carousel** component that cycles through slides endlessly in both directions.

### Requirements

- Accept a `slides` array of `{ id, content }` objects
- Display one slide at a time with **Previous** and **Next** navigation buttons
- Clicking Next on the **last** slide wraps to the **first** slide
- Clicking Previous on the **first** slide wraps to the **last** slide
- Display a **dot indicator** row — one dot per slide, with the active dot highlighted (class `carousel__dot--active`)
- Clicking a dot navigates directly to that slide
- Support an **autoPlay** prop (default `false`). When true, auto-advance every `interval` ms (default 3000)
- Auto-play pauses on mouse hover over the carousel and resumes on mouse leave
- Show a slide counter: `"{current} / {total}"`

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Carousel root | `carousel` |
| Current slide | `carousel-slide` |
| Previous button | `carousel-prev` |
| Next button | `carousel-next` |
| Dot at index N | `carousel-dot-{index}` (0-indexed) |
| Slide counter | `carousel-counter` |
constraints:
- Use React functional components with hooks
- Do not use any carousel/slider libraries
- The carousel must clean up intervals on unmount
hints: Track the active slide index in state. Use modular arithmetic for wrapping: (index + 1) % total.
For auto-play, use useEffect with setInterval. Return a cleanup function that calls clearInterval.
Use onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave to set a paused flag. The interval callback should check the flag before advancing.
For going previous with wrapping: (index - 1 + total) % total.
